enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Paarkaadhey'?

The song 'Paarkaadhey' expresses deep romantic feelings and longing, beautifully articulated through Vairamuthu's lyrics.

What is the context of this song in the movie 'Ambikapathy'?

In Ambikapathy, 'Paarkaadhey' plays during a pivotal moment that highlights the protagonist's emotional journey and love.

Who is the singer and music director of 'Paarkaadhey'?

The song is sung by AR Rahman, who is also the music director, showcasing his exceptional talent in both singing and composing.

When was 'Paarkaadhey' released?

'Paarkaadhey' was released on June 23, 2013, as part of the Ambikapathy soundtrack.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In 'Paarkaadhey', the protagonist expresses his deep feelings of love and longing, encapsulated in the soothing melody. The song serves as a backdrop to a significant moment in the movie Ambikapathy, where emotions run high. With AR Rahman's soulful rendition and Vairamuthu's evocative lyrics, it evokes a sense of nostalgia and romance. This beautiful composition resonates with anyone who has experienced the complexities of love.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Paarkaadhey from the movie Ambikapathy is a mesmerizing melody that captures the essence of romance and longing. With the enchanting voice of AR Rahman and the poetic lyrics by Vairamuthu, this song takes listeners on an emotional journey. Experience the magic of this collaboration that beautifully blends heartfelt emotions with captivating music.
stanzaMeanings: Look at me like this, just once (2) I have been watching until now, like this (2)
Look at me like this, just once (2)
I heard the pearls in the moonlight. Oh great queen, I heard your beauty. Oh, I heard about half of life. A flower's smile is all I heard.
Look at me like this, just once (2)
In the palace, I cried tears. Searching for that, you found a diamond. Oh love, you burned in the fire. A diamond shines in the sky, and I am lost in joy.
Look at me like this, just once (2) I have been watching until now, like this (2) I have been looking until now, like this (2)
Look at me like this, just once (2)
